items.idea: .raw contains its own format spec: header tokens, status whitelist, required keys, indentation, comment syntax
raw_loader hardcodes only minimal bootstrap grammar (kind tokens + indent rules); rest read from .raw
Chicken-egg solution: first section of .raw = `grammar` block (bootstrap-parseable), remaining sections parsed via those grammar rules
Alternative: give up pure self-host, adopt 'minimal trusted parser' model — declare raw_loader as legal trusted base; .raw includes rule 'raw_loader change requires rebootstrap ceremony'
Single orchestrator tool/raw_all.hexa — reads enforce/proof field from each rule, dispatches
Rule type taxonomy: file_presence, file_absence, content_match, content_absence, dir_structure (F5/F6/depth), proof_script, declarative_decl, cross_rule_consistency
Rule DAG with topological dispatch (raw#15 roadmap_lint depends on raw#9 proof-carrying). Upstream fail → skip downstream to cut cascade noise
Output format: PASS/FAIL/SKIP/WARN per rule + JSON mode for CI/IDE integration
Parallel dispatch for independent rules; sequential for DAG-dependent
Honest framing: macOS uchg is user-removable (no sudo needed). Real protection = ceremony-friendly friction, NOT attack surface. Do not lie about this
Blessed unlock path: tool/hx_unlock.hexa is the only caller of chflags nouchg. Requires raw_all exit 0 + mandatory --reason + audit log
Time-bounded window: auto re-lock daemon after 300s; forgetting is safe
Discourage raw chflags: document 'raw chflags forbidden — use hx_unlock'; audit warns on direct chflags detection
Hardened option: macOS schg (system immutable flag) requires root + single-user mode to clear. Real kernel enforcement, but recovery cost high. Apply only to .raw; .own etc remain uchg
Cross-platform: Linux chattr +i (requires root). Equivalent enforcement; shell wrapper branches on OS
Monotonic strengthening: lower layers can ADD or STRENGTHEN rules only; no weakening or removal
'No weakening' check: each .own rule with matching id must be subset/refinement of .raw rule
Scope narrowing: .raw scope='repo-wide' can be narrowed by .own to scope='self/' only, but never widened
Inheritance example: .raw 'no hardcoded' → .own 'no hardcoded paths in self/' + 'legacy/ explicit grandfather'
Multi-layer fail-fast: .raw fail → skip .own validation (if upper collapses, lower is meaningless)
raw_loader source is editable → a patch skipping validation defeats the gate
Lock scope expansion: .raw, .own, .ext, .roadmap, .loop + raw_loader.hexa + raw_all.hexa + hx_unlock.hexa + os_level_lock.hexa + build/hexa_stage0* — all uchg
Dev inconvenience becomes paper trail: any infrastructure edit requires unlock ceremony → git history records WHY
Circular rebuild already solved: .own rule 'rebuild_stage0.hexa must be path for stage0 changes'
File sha256 → last-known-good state table in .raw-cache/
Re-validate only changed files (of the 5 SSOT)
Fast path (format-only, <100ms) vs Full path (with proof scripts, a few seconds)
Unlock requires fast path only; lock runs full path — minimize unlock latency
sudo chflags nouchg .raw always works — official escape, must be documented
Emergency script: tool/emergency/raw_reset.sh — restore .raw from git HEAD + unlock (last-good guarantee)
Audit tag: emergency invocations get EMERGENCY tag in .raw-audit + prompt for reason
Recovery runbook: 'What to do when raw_loader is broken' procedure doc
Per-rule exemption file: .raw-exemptions/{rule_id}.list
Normalized exemption entry: {file, line_range, reason, expiry} — expiry date REQUIRED (no indefinite grandfather)
raw_all warns on expiring exemptions; auto-escalates to FAIL after expiry to force renewal or fix
Existing 5 status: live, warn, new, deferred, convention
Proposed addition: deprecated, retired — rule retirement flow
Promotion flow: 'new' → all violations fixed → auto-propose 'live' promotion
'warn' tier: weaker than 'live', does not block, stderr warnings only
Each rule's proof field: path to hexa script OR literal condition expression
Script proof: ./build/hexa_stage0 scripts/proof_X.hexa exit 0 = PASS
Literal proof: inline expression (e.g., count(shared/**/*) == 0 for shared-elimination proof)
Proof itself is validated: if a proof script violates raw rules, the proof is invalid
.raw-audit append-only log — unlock/lock/violation/emergency events
Format: timestamp | event | actor | reason | result | file_hash_before | file_hash_after
.raw-audit itself locked (uchg); only raw_all can append (via unlock wrapper)
Rotation: .raw-audit.{YYYY-MM} monthly rotation; past logs readonly
flock on .raw-lock during unlock window — prevent concurrent unlocks
Second unlock attempt: fail-fast with 'already unlocked' message (preferred over queuing)
Actor tagging: --actor=me or --actor=agent-N distinguishes unlock sources in audit
.raw = cross-project rules → shareable across repos
Strategy: canonical .raw in hexa-lang; other repos reference via .raw-ref (path/hash) + local copy
Drift detection: tool/raw_sync.hexa reports canonical-vs-local diff
.own is always per-repo independent
test/regression/raw_all_self.hexa — raw_all validates own repo (round-trip)
test/regression/raw_all_bad.hexa — seeded broken .raw fixtures → expected FAIL
test/regression/unlock_gate.hexa — attempt unlock with faked fail state → expected block
test/regression/inheritance.hexa — .own attempts to weaken .raw rule → expected FAIL
Common commands: ./hx unlock --reason '...' / ./hx lock / ./hx verify
Editor wrapper: ./hx edit .raw = unlock → $EDITOR .raw → lock on exit
IDE LSP: run raw_all on save, inline-render violations
./hx status shows currently-locked files + last verification result + expiring exemptions
OS-level gate = local friction; CI = team-wide block
CI job: raw_all --strict --no-lock (validate only, never touch chflags)
PRs touching .raw trigger special review (infrastructure-change warning)
.raw has version: field (currently 17 rules = v2 or similar)
raw_loader declares supported version range — old loader + new .raw = refuse with clear error
Migration tool: tool/raw_migrate.hexa v1_to_v2.hexa — automated upgrade path

items.label: this week
items.idea: tool/raw_all.hexa orchestrator + integrate existing 5 enforcers + single exit code
tool/hx_unlock.hexa wrapper — requires raw_all pass + audit log
.own layer validator — monotonic strengthening check
Time-bounded unlock window + re-lock daemon
Proof artifacts — each rule's proof field actually consumed
Exemption system + expiry management
Audit trail + .raw-audit permanent log
Cache layer (sha256)
Rule DAG + topological dispatch
Emergency recovery kit
LSP / IDE / CI integration
Multi-repo propagation + canonical .raw sync
raw_loader bug → false FAIL → permanent lockout. Mitigation: emergency kit + sudo fallback
Infinite recursion (raw_all validates raw_all). Mitigation: explicit fixpoint (self-validation depth capped at 1)
chflags technical limits (macOS only; uchg user-removable). Mitigation: no lying, frame as friction model
Developer fatigue — unlock ceremony on every edit is annoying. Mitigation: editor wrapper + 300s auto re-lock
Exemption abuse → rule becomes dead letter. Mitigation: mandatory expiry + audit warnings for long-standing exemptions
'Rules are data, validators are pure functions' — changing a rule = editing data; logic changes minimal
'Rule as SSOT' — rules live in one place (.raw). Validator scripts just read them
'Kernel as trust anchor' — OS politely locks for us. What we build is ceremony
'Paper trail over prevention' — perfect blocking impossible. Who-when-why audit is the real asset
'Proof-carrying rules' — every rule must either carry a proof artifact or be labeled convention-only

next_action.description: tool/raw_all.hexa orchestrator that unifies existing 5 enforcers under single entry with per-rule dispatch and aggregated exit code
next_action.rationale: Minimal viable pivot — unlocks P0b (hx_unlock) + P0c (.own layer) + P0d (timed window) in sequence
